previous_statement: (Artin-Wedderburn decomposition of commutant). Since π_φ(A)' is a finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra (equivalently, a finite-dimensional C*-algebra), by the Artin-Wedderburn theorem it is *-isomorphic to a direct sum of full matrix algebras: π_φ(A)' ≅ M_{n_1}(C) ⊕ M_{n_2}(C) ⊕ ... ⊕ M_{n_k}(C) for unique integers n_1, ..., n_k ≥ 1 (up to reordering). Here k is the number of minimal central projections in π_φ(A)'.
new_statement: (Artin-Wedderburn decomposition of commutant — amended). By Node 1.4.1, π_φ(A)' is a finite-dimensional C*-algebra (finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra). By the Artin-Wedderburn theorem (Lam, 'A First Course in Noncommutative Rings', Theorem 3.5; or Kadison-Ringrose Vol. I, Theorem 6.6.5 for the C*-version), combined with the Gelfand-Mazur theorem (the only finite-dimensional complex division algebra is C): π_φ(A)' ≅ M_{n_1}(C) ⊕ M_{n_2}(C) ⊕ ... ⊕ M_{n_k}(C) for unique integers n_1, ..., n_k ≥ 1 (up to reordering). Here k is the number of minimal central projections in π_φ(A)'. This is an application of the Artin-Wedderburn theorem (external reference), depending on Node 1.4.1.
